title: GoDaddy (GDDY) Economic Prospect Score — Opus 4.6 Analysis
description: Opus 4.6 forward-looking economic prospect analysis of GoDaddy Inc. (GDDY).
verdictDetail: GoDaddy has evolved from a domain registrar into a commerce-enabled platform for small businesses, and this transition is working. The company manages 84M+ domains and serves 21M+ customers, making it the world's largest web presence provider. The Applications & Commerce segment (website builders, email marketing, POS, payments) is growing mid-teens and now represents nearly half of revenue. GoDaddy's GoDaddy Airo AI-powered website builder is genuinely impressive — it creates functional small business websites in minutes. Margin expansion has been exceptional as the company shifts from a marketing-heavy domain business to a SaaS-like recurring model. The risk is TAM ceiling — GoDaddy serves micro-businesses (1-10 employees) that have high churn rates.

competitiveMomentum.title: Competitive Momentum
competitiveMomentum.summary: GoDaddy is executing a successful platform transition with accelerating commerce revenue and expanding margins. Domain revenue is stable and generates cash to fund growth.

factors.name: Revenue Growth vs. Peers
factors.rationale: FY2025 revenue grew ~8% to ~$4.6B, with Applications & Commerce growing ~14% and Core Platform (domains/hosting) growing ~3%. This outpaces Wix on total revenue but trails Shopify's growth rate. For a company GoDaddy's size, high-single-digit growth with margin expansion is attractive.

factors.name: Market Share Trajectory
factors.rationale: GoDaddy holds ~20% global domain registrar share — dominant but stable. In small business website builders, GoDaddy competes with Wix, Squarespace, and WordPress but has the largest customer base. GoDaddy Payments is gaining traction with ~$35B in annualized payment volume.

factors.name: Pricing Power
factors.rationale: Domain registrations have regulated wholesale pricing (ICANN fees) with limited markup potential. However, the commerce and applications stack carries SaaS-like pricing with annual escalators. GoDaddy's strategy of landing customers on cheap domains and upselling higher-margin services is effective.

factors.name: Product Velocity
factors.rationale: GoDaddy Airo (AI website builder) is a standout product that significantly reduces the friction of creating a web presence. GoDaddy Payments integration is progressing. However, the pace of new feature development still trails Shopify and Wix in many areas. The company is catching up, not leading.

moatDurability.title: Moat Durability
moatDurability.summary: GoDaddy's moat rests on brand recognition, domain portfolio lock-in, and the friction of migrating a small business's web presence. It's meaningful but not impenetrable.

factors.name: Switching Costs
factors.rationale: Moving a domain is technically easy but psychologically daunting for non-technical small business owners. Moving an entire web presence (website, email, commerce, payments) is genuinely difficult. GoDaddy's strategy of bundling services increases switching friction materially. Once a business is running on GoDaddy's commerce stack, migration is a multi-week project.

factors.name: Network Effects
factors.rationale: Modest network effects from GoDaddy's marketplace of web professionals and the data advantages of serving 21M+ customers. The AI tools improve with more customer data. But fundamentally, web hosting and commerce are not network-effect businesses.

factors.name: Regulatory & IP Position
factors.rationale: Domain registration is regulated by ICANN, creating standard barriers that apply to all registrars. GoDaddy's brand is its primary IP asset — it's the most recognized name in domain registration globally. No significant patent moat.

factors.name: Capital Intensity Advantage
factors.rationale: GoDaddy runs an efficient cloud-based platform with ~30% free cash flow margins. Capex is modest (~4% of revenue). The combination of recurring revenue and low capital requirements generates attractive ROIC and supports aggressive share buybacks.

sentimentCatalyst.title: Sentiment & Catalysts
sentimentCatalyst.summary: Sentiment has turned increasingly positive as the margin expansion story plays out and AI features differentiate the product. The stock has significantly re-rated over the past 18 months.

factors.name: Earnings Estimate Revisions
factors.rationale: FY2026 EPS estimates have been revised up ~15% over the past year as margin expansion exceeds expectations. The Street now models 15%+ EPS growth with expanding free cash flow margins approaching 35%. GoDaddy has consistently beaten estimates.

factors.name: News & Narrative Sentiment
factors.rationale: The narrative has shifted from 'domain commodity business' to 'AI-powered SMB platform with expanding margins.' GoDaddy Airo's AI capabilities generate positive press. The concern is whether AI tools (making website creation trivial) ultimately commoditize GoDaddy's own value proposition.

factors.name: Management & Capital Allocation
factors.rationale: CEO Aman Bhutani has executed the platform transition admirably since 2019. Capital allocation is shareholder-friendly — $3B+ in buybacks over the past 3 years, reducing share count ~25%. Minimal M&A, focused on organic execution. The approach is disciplined and working.

keyRisks: Micro-business churn: GoDaddy's customer base (1-10 employee businesses) has inherently high failure rates, creating a churn headwind that requires constant new customer acquisition to overcome
AI commoditization: as AI makes website creation trivially easy across all platforms, GoDaddy's core value proposition may be commoditized — if anyone can build a site in minutes, why pay GoDaddy?
Competition from integrated ecosystems: Shopify, Square, and even social media platforms (Instagram Shopping) are encroaching on GoDaddy's small business commerce TAM from different angles
keyCatalysts: Commerce attach rate expansion: only ~5% of GoDaddy's 21M+ customers use commerce tools — increasing penetration to 10% would nearly double Applications & Commerce revenue
GoDaddy Payments scaling toward $50B+ annualized volume provides a high-margin revenue stream that monetizes the existing customer base without additional acquisition cost
AI-driven ARPU expansion: Airo and other AI tools enable GoDaddy to offer higher-value services (marketing automation, SEO optimization) that increase revenue per customer
methodology: Opus 4.6 Analysis — Economic Prospect Score based on three pillars: Competitive Momentum (0-35), Moat Durability (0-35), and Sentiment & Catalysts (0-30).
